title: King-Size Framed Upholstered Headboard
summary: A king headboard with a built-up wood frame and legs surrounding a padded, fabric-wrapped panel — carpentry and upholstery in one build.
description:
This headboard reads as an expensive upholstered piece but is mostly a plywood back with layered 1x3 trim and store-bought molding forming a frame, and a single foam-and-fabric panel dropped into the opening. The wood frame does the structural and visual work; the upholstery is one afternoon of foam, fabric, and a staple gun.

One thing to watch: the source's own diagrams overstate several lengths, and the corrected notes matter. The back is 76-1/2" x 40", the bottom and top rails are 76-1/2" and 78" (not the 82-1/2"/84" the drawings show), and the fabric panel is about 35" x 71-1/2" — the diagram's panel length runs 6" long. Cut to the corrected numbers, not the pictures.

Build it in layers: back, then the bottom-layer trim, then the bottom rail and legs and top, then the mitered molding frame, and finally the padded panel. Miter the molding corners cleanly at 45° — an upholstered field forgives a lot, but a gapped miter right next to it draws the eye. Dry-fit the panel before you upholster and leave a little clearance around it for the foam and fabric to take up, or the wrapped panel won't drop back into its opening.

tools.note: Breaks down the sheet goods; a store rip is fine for the big panel.
All the trim and the mitered molding frame go on with glue and brads.
Wraps the foam and fabric onto the panel.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood or MDF, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Back panel. Builder's grade is fine; just make sure it's flat.

materials.name: 1x3 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Trim layers, bottom, legs, and top.

materials.name: 2-1/2" unfinished molding, 10 ft
materials.note: The mitered frame around the panel.

materials.name: Fabric, 45"+ wide
materials.note: Covers the panel; choose a mid-weight upholstery fabric.

materials.name: Wood screws, 1" and 2"
materials.note: 1" to screw the panel to the back; 2" to fasten the headboard to the bed or wall.

cutList.note: Approximate — dry-fit and trim to the opening, leaving room for foam and fabric.
Cut to fit the opening, mitered 45° at the corners.

steps.title: Cut the back to size
steps.body: Cut (or have cut) the 3/4" back to 76-1/2" x 40". Builder's-grade plywood is fine here since it's fully covered, but pick a flat, undamaged sheet — a bowed back telegraphs through everything.

steps.title: Add the bottom-layer trim
steps.body: Fasten the top and bottom trim (1x3 at 76-1/2"), then the side trim, to the back with 1-1/4" nails and glue, keeping outside edges flush. This layer builds the frame's depth.

steps.title: Bottom rail, legs, and top
steps.body: Fasten the bottom rail (76-1/2", not the diagram's 82-1/2") with 2" nails and glue into both the back and the trim layer; the front overhangs 1". Add the legs, then the top rail (78", not 84"), nailing into the legs, top trim, and back. Fasten the leg fronts through the sides and to the headboard top.

steps.title: Mitered molding frame
steps.body: Measure and cut the store-bought molding to frame the opening, mitering the corners at 45°. Glue and nail it with 1-1/4" nails. Fill nail holes and any miter gaps, sand, and finish the wood now — before the fabric goes in, so you don't get finish on the upholstery.

steps.title: Upholster and install the panel
steps.body: Cut the 1/2" panel to about 35" x 71-1/2" and dry-fit it in the opening, leaving clearance for foam and fabric. Lay foam on the panel, wrap the fabric around, and staple it taut on the back — pull corners neatly so they don't bunch. Set the wrapped panel into the opening and screw it to the back from behind with 1" screws.

images.alt: King-size headboard with a wood frame around a padded fabric panel
